questions.question: In a content creation pipeline, what does the Research Agent do?
options: Edits the draft for grammar and clarity
Finds and gathers relevant information on the given topic
Formats the final output as HTML
Scores the quality of the finished article
questions.explanation: The Research Agent is responsible for sourcing facts, links, and context — it uses search tools to gather the raw material that the Writer Agent will turn into content.

questions.question: What is the Coordinator's role in a multi-agent pipeline?
options: It writes the first draft of the content
It stores all research results in a vector database
It orchestrates the workflow, directing agents in the right sequence
It validates the final output against a rubric
questions.explanation: The Coordinator (supervisor) manages the pipeline — it decides which agent runs next, passes outputs between agents, and ensures the overall goal is met.

questions.question: Why separate the Writer and Editor into distinct agents?
options: Separation of concerns — creation and critique benefit from different perspectives
Because LangChain requires at least three agents
To reduce the total number of tokens used
So they can run on different machines
questions.explanation: Having separate Writer and Editor agents mirrors how human workflows operate — one agent focuses on generating content freely while another applies critical scrutiny without self-censorship bias.
